name: setup
description: Install QMD and configure collections for your workspace. This sets up local hybrid search (BM25 + vector + re-ranking) replacing expensive API-based memory searches. Run this first after installing the skill. Creates collections based on workspace structure (workspace, daily-logs, intelligence, projects).

name: calculate_savings
description: Calculate how much you'll save in API costs by using QMD local search instead of cloud-based memory APIs. Prompts for daily search frequency and average cost per call, then shows monthly and annual savings. Use this to understand the ROI of switching to local search.

cost_per_call.description: Average cost per API memory call in dollars

name: refresh
description: Update QMD search index and regenerate embeddings. Use this after adding new files to your workspace to ensure they're searchable. Runs qmd update (rebuilds index) and qmd embed (regenerates vector embeddings). For scheduled updates, a cron job is set up automatically during setup.

name: serve
description: Start QMD as a shared MCP server for multi-agent setups. This enables multiple agents (Forge, Thoth, Axis) to share a collective memory knowledge base. Returns the server status and connection info. For single-agent use, this is not required.

name: add_collection
description: Add a new collection to your QMD index. Collections group related documents for searching. Common use cases: add research folders, project directories, or specific document types. Each collection can have its own file mask (e.g., *.md, **/*.txt).

path.description: Path to the directory to add as collection

name: add_context
description: Add a natural language description to a collection to improve search quality. Context helps QMD understand what type of content is in each collection, improving relevance of hybrid search results. Use this after adding new collections.

context.description: Description of what this collection contains (e.g., 'Market research and competitive analysis')

name: apply_template
description: Apply a workspace template to quickly set up optimized collections for your use case. Templates create pre-configured collection structures. Options: trading (intelligence, market-data, research, daily-logs), content (articles, drafts, research, ideas), developer (docs, notes, decisions, snippets).

template.description: Template to apply: 'trading' for investors, 'content' for creators, 'developer' for engineers

name: qmd_search
description: Fast keyword search using BM25 algorithm. Best for exact matches and when you know specific terms. Much faster than semantic search but doesn't understand concepts. Use this for finding exact mentions, proper nouns, or when speed matters. For conceptual similarity, use qmd_vsearch. For best quality, use qmd_query.

collection.description: Specific collection to search (optional, searches all if not specified)

name: qmd_vsearch
description: Semantic vector search for conceptual similarity. Uses local embeddings (GGUF models) to find content conceptually related to your query, even if exact terms don't match. Slower than keyword search but understands meaning. Best for: 'how should we handle X', 'what decisions about Y'. For exact matches use qmd_search. For best quality use qmd_query.

name: qmd_query
description: Hybrid search with query expansion and LLM re-ranking. This is the recommended search mode - combines BM25 keyword matching, semantic vector search, and re-ranks results using a local LLM for best relevance. Slower but highest quality. Use this for complex queries, important research, or when you need the most accurate results.

query.description: Search query (can be natural language question)
